title: dryopteris microchlaena
intro: Dryopteris microchlaena, also known as the fern-like plant of the Dryopteris genus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris microchlaena include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to improve digestion and reduce inflammation. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or decoction, and can also be applied topically to the skin in the form of a poultice or ointment.
